hook.text: Pierre de Fermat discovered a shortcut for enormous calculations using primes.

buildup.text: His little theorem says: raise any number to a prime power, and the remainder follows a rule.

discovery.text: Specifically, a to the power p mod p always equals a mod p, when p is prime.

twist.text: Fermat never published a full proof. Euler completed it decades later.

climax.text: This theorem is the backbone of RSA encryption, protecting billions of transactions.

punchline.text: A 17th-century trick now guards the internet.

quiz.question: What does Fermat's Little Theorem help simplify?
